seoTitle: Mitsubishi Electric Products | DIPIPM, IGBT Module, SiC MOSFET | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Browse Mitsubishi Electric products including DIPIPMs, IGBT modules, IPMs, and SiC MOSFETs. Technical support available for home appliances, industrial drives, and EV applications.

faqs.question: What product categories does Mitsubishi Electric offer?
faqs.answer: Mitsubishi Electric offers four main product categories: IGBT Modules including NX series and standard series for high-power industrial applications; DIPIPMs for home appliance motor control with over 100 million units shipped; IPM Modules for industrial intelligent power applications; and SiC MOSFETs for next-generation high-efficiency systems. Each category covers voltage ranges from 600V to 1700V to meet diverse application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories below or contact our FAE team for selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Mitsubishi Electric product for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Mitsubishi product involves: (1) Identify your application requirements - voltage rating, current capacity, switching frequency, and package type. (2) Review the product category that matches your needs - DIPIPM for home appliances, IGBT modules for industrial drives, or SiC for highest efficiency. (3) Compare specifications using our parametric tables. (4) Check reference designs and application notes. (5) Contact our FAE team for technical consultation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for personalized product recommendations based on your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of Mitsubishi DIPIPM technology?
faqs.answer: Mitsubishi DIPIPM technology provides several advantages: (1) Market proven - Over 100 million units shipped worldwide. (2) Highly integrated - Built-in HVIC, bootstrap diodes, and comprehensive protection. (3) Cost-effective - Reduces external component count and design time. (4) Compact size - Standard dual-in-line package. (5) Easy design - Minimal external components required. (6) Wide voltage range - 600V to 1200V options. (7) Proven reliability - Billions of operating hours in field applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use DIPIPM for home appliance applications up to 2.2kW requiring compact, cost-effective solutions.

categories.name: IGBT Modules
categories.description: Mitsubishi IGBT modules include NX series and standard series for high-power industrial drives and renewable energy applications.
categories.longDescription: Mitsubishi Electric's IGBT module portfolio features the advanced NX series and standard series, designed for high-power industrial applications. The NX series incorporates 7th generation CSTBT (Carrier Stored Trench-gate Bipolar Transistor) technology, achieving industry-leading efficiency with low Vce(sat) and fast switching characteristics. Voltage ratings from 600V to 1700V and current ratings from 100A to 1400A cover applications from servo drives to large industrial inverters and renewable energy systems. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive IGBT Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.description: 7th generation CSTBT technology for highest efficiency

series.description: Proven IGBT technology for general purpose applications

selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting IGBT modules for your application

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Excellent igbt modules component for industrial applications. Reliable performance and robust design characteristics make it suitable for demanding environments.

faqs.question: What is CSTBT technology in Mitsubishi IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: CSTBT (Carrier Stored Trench-gate Bipolar Transistor) is Mitsubishi's proprietary IGBT technology: (1) Carrier storage layer - Optimized carrier injection reduces Vce(sat) while maintaining fast switching; (2) Trench-gate structure - Improves channel density and reduces on-resistance; (3) 7th generation - Latest technology with 15-20% lower losses than previous generation; (4) Soft switching characteristics - Reduces EMI and voltage spikes; (5) High reliability - Proven in demanding industrial applications. The CM300DX-24S1 uses this technology for best-in-class performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose CSTBT technology for applications requiring highest efficiency and reliability.

faqs.question: How do I calculate power losses for the CM300DX-24S1?
faqs.answer: Power loss calculation for CM300DX-24S1: (1) Conduction loss = Vce(sat) × Ic × duty cycle. At 225A RMS with 1.7V Vce(sat) and 80% modulation: Pcond = 1.7 × 225 × 0.8 = 306W per IGBT, 612W total. (2) Switching loss = (Eon + Eoff) × fsw. At 8kHz with typical Eon=25mJ, Eoff=35mJ: Psw = 60mJ × 8kHz = 480W total. (3) Total loss = 1092W. (4) For thermal design with Rth(j-c)=0.06°C/W and Rth(c-s)=0.1°C/W, heatsink thermal resistance should be <0.12°C/W to keep Tj<150°C at 50°C ambient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate losses at maximum operating conditions; design thermal management for worst-case scenario.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is recommended for CM300DX-24S1?
faqs.answer: Recommended gate drive for CM300DX-24S1: (1) Turn-on voltage: +15V ± 0.5V for full conduction. (2) Turn-off voltage: -5V to -8V for reliable turn-off and dv/dt immunity. (3) Gate resistance: 2.2Ω to 6.8Ω depending on switching speed requirements. Lower resistance for faster switching (higher EMI), higher resistance for slower switching (lower EMI). (4) Gate drive power: approximately 3W per IGBT at 8kHz. (5) Use isolated gate drivers with >2500Vrms isolation. (6) Implement active Miller clamp for high dv/dt applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +15V/-8V gate drive for industrial applications; adjust gate resistance based on EMI requirements.

faqs.question: Can CM300DX-24S1 be used for solar inverter applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, CM300DX-24S1 is excellent for solar inverters: (1) Voltage rating - 1200V suitable for 1000V DC bus commercial systems. (2) Current rating - 300A supports inverters up to 150kW. (3) Efficiency - Low Vce(sat) and fast switching enable >98% inverter efficiency. (4) Reliability - Proven in outdoor solar applications with wide temperature range. (5) Thermal performance - Excellent for high ambient temperature operation. (6) Cost-effective - Competitive pricing for high-power applications. Recommended for three-phase commercial solar inverters 50kW-150kW.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for commercial solar inverters; consider parallel operation for higher power.

faqs.question: What protection features should be implemented with CM300DX-24S1?
faqs.answer: Recommended protection for CM300DX-24S1: (1) Overcurrent protection - desaturation detection with <5μs response time. (2) Short-circuit protection - soft turn-off to prevent overvoltage spikes. (3) Overvoltage protection - active clamping or TVS diodes for inductive spikes. (4) Overtemperature protection - monitor NTC thermistor, shutdown at 125°C heatsink. (5) Undervoltage lockout - disable gate drive if Vge < 12V. (6) Shoot-through protection - hardware interlock and dead-time control. (7) Gate protection - TVS diodes to protect against overvoltage (>20V).
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement all protections for reliable operation; desaturation detection is essential for short-circuit protection.

faqs.question: What is the maximum switching frequency for CM300DX-24S1?
faqs.answer: Maximum switching frequency depends on thermal constraints: (1) Recommended operating frequency: 2-15kHz for industrial drives. (2) Maximum practical frequency: 20kHz with adequate cooling. (3) Higher frequencies increase switching losses significantly - at 20kHz, switching losses are 2.5x higher than at 8kHz. (4) For high-frequency operation (>15kHz), consider SiC hybrid modules. (5) Thermal simulation recommended for frequencies above 10kHz. The CM300DX-24S1 is optimized for 2-10kHz operation typical of industrial motor drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 2-10kHz for standard industrial drives; consider SiC for frequencies above 15kHz.

faqs.question: What applications is the CM600HU-12F best suited for?
faqs.answer: The CM600HU-12F is optimized for: (1) UPS systems - High current capability for online UPS 100kVA-200kVA. (2) Welding machines - High surge current for arc welding applications. (3) Power supplies - High-current DC power supplies for industrial use. (4) Motor drives - High-power motor control up to 300kW. (5) Battery chargers - Fast charging systems for industrial batteries. The 600V rating supports 380V AC systems with adequate margin. The high current rating provides excellent overload capability for demanding applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for high-current applications 300A-600A; ideal for UPS and welding machines.

faqs.question: How does the CM600HU-12F handle surge currents?
faqs.answer: CM600HU-12F surge current capability: (1) Pulse rating - Can handle 1200A peak current for short durations. (2) I²t rating - High I²t for fuse coordination and protection. (3) Robust design - Thick copper metallization handles high current density. (4) Thermal mass - Large silicon area provides thermal inertia for short overloads. (5) Safe operating area - Wide SOA ensures reliable operation under overload conditions. (6) Protection coordination - Designed to work with standard fuses and circuit breakers. The module is ideal for applications with high inrush currents like welding and motor starting.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify surge requirements against pulse ratings; coordinate protection devices properly.

faqs.question: What is the difference between NX series and Standard series?
faqs.answer: NX series vs Standard series comparison: (1) Technology - NX uses 7th generation CSTBT; Standard uses proven 6th generation IGBT. (2) Efficiency - NX has 10-15% lower losses than Standard. (3) Switching speed - NX has faster switching with better softness. (4) Applications - NX for high-efficiency drives; Standard for cost-sensitive applications. (5) Price - NX approximately 15-20% higher cost. (6) Availability - Both series have good availability. (7) Package - Same mechanical dimensions for easy substitution. The CM600HU-12F Standard series offers excellent value for applications where ultimate efficiency is not critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose NX for highest efficiency; Standard for cost-sensitive applications.

faqs.question: How do I parallel CM600HU-12F modules?
faqs.answer: CM600HU-12F paralleling guidelines: (1) Static current sharing - Select modules from same production lot with matched Vce(sat). (2) Dynamic current sharing - Symmetric layout with equal collector and emitter inductance. (3) Gate drive - Common gate drive with individual gate resistors (2-5Ω each). (4) Layout - Parallel at DC terminals with short, wide busbars. (5) Current sharing - Expect 10-15% current imbalance due to parameter variation. (6) Derating - Parallel 3 modules for 2.5x current capability. (7) Testing - Verify current sharing with current probes under full load. With proper design, paralleled CM600HU-12Fs can handle 1500A+ continuous current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use modules from same lot; implement symmetric layout; verify current sharing.

faqs.question: What cooling is required for CM600HU-12F?
faqs.answer: Cooling requirements for CM600HU-12F: (1) Thermal resistance Rth(j-c) = 0.08°C/W per IGBT. (2) For 400A RMS output at 8kHz: total losses approximately 800W. (3) Heatsink calculation: Rth(s-a) = (Tjmax - Ta)/P - Rth(j-c) - Rth(c-s) = (150-50)/800 - 0.08 - 0.15 = 0.04°C/W. (4) Practical heatsink: liquid cooling with thermal resistance <0.05°C/W or large forced air heatsink. (5) Thermal interface: high-performance thermal grease or phase change material. (6) Mounting: 4 screws with 2.5-3.5 N·m torque. (7) For high current operation, liquid cooling is recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: High current operation requires liquid cooling; forced air for moderate currents.

faqs.question: Can CM600HU-12F be used for welding machine applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, CM600HU-12F is excellent for welding machines: (1) Surge current - High pulse current capability handles arc starting transients. (2) Robustness - Designed for harsh welding environment with high reliability. (3) Voltage rating - 600V suitable for 380V input welding power supplies. (4) Current rating - 600A supports high-power welding up to 500A output. (5) Thermal performance - Handles high duty cycles typical of welding. (6) Short-circuit capability - 10μs withstand time for arc fault conditions. (7) Field proven - Widely used in industrial welding equipment. Recommended for MMA, MIG/MAG, and TIG welding power supplies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for high-power welding machines; excellent surge current capability.

categories.name: DIPIPM
categories.description: Mitsubishi DIPIPMs are the industry-leading intelligent power modules for home appliance motor control with over 100 million units shipped worldwide.
categories.longDescription: Mitsubishi Electric's DIPIPM (Dual-In-Line Package Intelligent Power Module) series is the world's most popular intelligent power module for home appliance applications. Featuring built-in HVIC (High Voltage Integrated Circuit) for single-supply operation, bootstrap diodes, and comprehensive protection functions, DIPIPMs significantly reduce external component count and design time. With over 100 million units shipped, DIPIPMs have proven reliability in billions of operating hours. The series covers voltage ratings from 600V to 1200V and current ratings from 5A to 50A, making them ideal for air conditioners, refrigerators, washing machines, and other home appliances up to 2.2kW. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive DIPIPM selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.description: Higher current rating for larger appliances

faqs.question: What makes DIPIPM ideal for home appliance applications?
faqs.answer: DIPIPM is ideal for home appliances because: (1) Highly integrated - Built-in HVIC, bootstrap diodes, and protection reduce external components by 70%. (2) Single-supply operation - +15V only, no negative supply needed. (3) Compact size - DIP package fits standard PCB layouts. (4) Cost-effective - Reduces BOM cost and assembly time. (5) Proven reliability - Over 100 million units shipped with billions of operating hours. (6) Easy design - Minimal external components and simple interface. (7) Comprehensive protection - UVLO, OT, and FO protect the system. These features make DIPIPM the industry standard for appliance motor control.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose DIPIPM for home appliances up to 2.2kW requiring compact, cost-effective solutions.

faqs.question: How do I design the bootstrap circuit for PS219A4-ASTX?
faqs.answer: Bootstrap circuit design for PS219A4-ASTX: (1) Bootstrap capacitor - Typically 10-47μF, 25V rating, low ESR ceramic or electrolytic. (2) Bootstrap diode - Built-in, no external diode needed. (3) Charging - Capacitor charges through internal diode when low-side IGBT is on. (4) Sizing - Calculate based on gate charge and switching frequency: Cboot > 2×(2Qg + Iq/fsw + Qls)/Vripple. (5) Placement - Place capacitor close to VB and VS pins. (6) Refresh - Ensure minimum low-side on-time for capacitor charging. (7) Voltage - Maintain 13-18V bootstrap voltage for reliable operation. The built-in bootstrap diode simplifies design significantly.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 22μF/25V bootstrap capacitor; place close to module; ensure adequate refresh time.

faqs.question: What protection functions are built into PS219A4-ASTX?
faqs.answer: PS219A4-ASTX built-in protection: (1) UVLO (Undervoltage Lockout) - Disables operation if VCC drops below threshold (typically 10.5V). (2) OT (Overtemperature) - Built-in temperature sensor shuts down at 145°C junction. (3) FO (Fault Output) - Open-drain output indicates fault condition. (4) Short-circuit protection - Soft turn-off limits current during short-circuit. (5) Cross-conduction prevention - Internal interlock prevents shoot-through. (6) Input filter - Built-in 1μs noise filter on control inputs. These protections ensure reliable operation in appliance applications without external protection circuits.
faqs.decisionGuide: All protections active by default; connect FO to MCU for fault monitoring.

faqs.question: Can PS219A4-ASTX be used for inverter air conditioners?
faqs.answer: Yes, PS219A4-ASTX is excellent for inverter air conditioners: (1) Current rating - 20A supports compressor motors up to 1.5kW. (2) Voltage rating - 600V suitable for 220V AC systems. (3) Switching frequency - Supports 5-15kHz carrier frequency for quiet operation. (4) Efficiency - Low losses enable high-efficiency inverter designs. (5) Reliability - Proven in millions of air conditioner installations. (6) Cost-effective - Reduces system cost compared to discrete solutions. (7) Compact - Fits in space-constrained outdoor units. Recommended for residential inverter air conditioners 0.5-1.5kW cooling capacity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for inverter air conditioners up to 1.5kW; consider higher current DIPIPM for larger units.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Super Mini and Large DIPIPM?
faqs.answer: Super Mini vs Large DIPIPM comparison: (1) Package size - Super Mini is 30% smaller than Large DIPIPM. (2) Current rating - Super Mini up to 30A; Large DIPIPM up to 50A. (3) Applications - Super Mini for small appliances; Large for high-power appliances. (4) Thermal performance - Large DIPIPM has better thermal characteristics. (5) Price - Super Mini more cost-effective for low-power applications. (6) Pin compatibility - Different pinouts, not interchangeable. (7) Availability - Both have good availability. Choose Super Mini for space-constrained designs; Large DIPIPM for higher power requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Super Mini for small appliances; Large DIPIPM for high-power applications.

faqs.question: How do I interface PS219A4-ASTX with my MCU?
faqs.answer: MCU interface for PS219A4-ASTX: (1) Control inputs - 6 inputs (HIN/LIN for each phase), 3.3V/5V logic compatible. (2) Input current - 1mA typical, can be driven directly by MCU. (3) Pull-down - Built-in pull-down resistors, no external needed. (4) Dead time - Insert 2-5μs dead time in MCU software. (5) FO output - Open-drain, connect to MCU with 10kΩ pull-up. (6) VFO - Fault output reference, connect to MCU VCC. (7) Isolation - No isolation needed between MCU and DIPIPM (HVIC provides isolation). Interface is very simple requiring only 6 GPIO pins and one pull-up resistor.
faqs.decisionGuide: Connect directly to MCU GPIO; add 2-5μs dead time in software; use pull-up on FO.

faqs.question: What is the maximum power for PS22A7A-ASTX applications?
faqs.answer: PS22A7A-ASTX power capability: (1) Continuous current - 30A at 25°C ambient. (2) Motor power - Supports compressor motors up to 2.2kW. (3) Application examples - 3HP air conditioners, large heat pumps, industrial fans. (4) Thermal design - Requires adequate heatsinking for continuous operation. (5) Overload - 150% for 60 seconds typical for compressor starting. (6) Voltage - 600V rating suitable for 220V AC systems. (7) Switching frequency - 5-15kHz for appliance applications. The module is ideal for high-power residential and light commercial appliances.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for appliances 1.5-2.2kW; ensure adequate thermal design.

faqs.question: How does the thermal performance compare to Super Mini DIPIPM?
faqs.answer: Large DIPIPM+ vs Super Mini thermal comparison: (1) Thermal resistance - Large DIPIPM+ has 20% lower Rth(j-c). (2) Current capability - 30A vs 20A for same temperature rise. (3) Package size - Large is 30% larger but better heat dissipation. (4) Heatsink requirements - Large DIPIPM+ requires less aggressive cooling. (5) Continuous operation - Better suited for high-duty-cycle applications. (6) Peak current - Higher pulse current capability. (7) Reliability - Lower junction temperatures improve long-term reliability. Choose Large DIPIPM+ for thermally challenging applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose Large DIPIPM+ for high-power or thermally challenging applications.

faqs.question: Can PS22A7A-ASTX be used for heat pump applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, PS22A7A-ASTX is excellent for heat pumps: (1) Power range - 30A supports heat pumps 2-3kW heating capacity. (2) Voltage - 600V rating suitable for 220V systems. (3) Temperature range - Operating temperature -20°C to +100°C for outdoor use. (4) Reliability - Proven in heat pump applications with wide temperature cycling. (5) Efficiency - High efficiency for year-round operation. (6) Protection - Comprehensive protection for compressor and fan control. (7) Cost-effective - Integrated solution reduces system cost. Recommended for residential and light commercial heat pumps.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for heat pumps up to 3kW; excellent for outdoor temperature extremes.

faqs.question: What cooling is required for PS22A7A-ASTX?
faqs.answer: Cooling requirements for PS22A7A-ASTX: (1) Thermal resistance Rth(j-c) = 2.5°C/W typical. (2) For 20A continuous: losses approximately 25W. (3) Heatsink calculation: Rth(s-a) = (Tjmax - Ta)/P - Rth(j-c) = (150-50)/25 - 2.5 = 1.5°C/W. (4) Practical solution - Small aluminum heatsink with natural or forced convection. (5) PCB copper - Use thick copper (2oz) for heat spreading. (6) Thermal interface - Thermal grease or pad between module and heatsink. (7) Mounting - Secure mounting with appropriate torque. For most appliance applications, a small heatsink or metal chassis mounting is sufficient.
faqs.decisionGuide: Small heatsink or chassis mounting sufficient for most applications.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency range for PS22A7A-ASTX?
faqs.answer: PS22A7A-ASTX switching frequency: (1) Recommended range - 5-15kHz for appliance motor control. (2) Minimum frequency - 1kHz (limited by bootstrap capacitor refresh). (3) Maximum frequency - 20kHz (thermal limitations). (4) Carrier frequency - 5-8kHz typical for quiet operation. (5) V/f control - Lower frequencies acceptable. (6) Vector control - Higher frequencies may be needed. (7) Trade-off - Higher frequency increases switching losses. For most appliances, 5-8kHz provides good balance of efficiency and audible noise.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 5-8kHz for quiet operation; up to 15kHz for vector control applications.

faqs.question: How does PS22A7A-ASTX handle motor starting currents?
faqs.answer: PS22A7A-ASTX motor starting capability: (1) Pulse current - 60A for 1ms non-repetitive. (2) Starting current - 150% of rated for 60 seconds. (3) I²t rating - High I²t for motor starting transients. (4) Protection - Soft start and current limiting protect module. (5) Thermal design - Account for starting losses in thermal design. (6) Compressor starting - Handles locked-rotor current for compressor starting. (7) Reliability - Designed for frequent starting cycles typical of appliances. The module is well-suited for compressor and fan motor starting requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Module handles typical motor starting; verify against specific motor requirements.

categories.name: IPM Modules
categories.description: Mitsubishi IPM modules provide intelligent power solutions for industrial applications with integrated gate drivers and comprehensive protection.
categories.longDescription: Mitsubishi Electric's IPM (Intelligent Power Module) series combines IGBT technology with advanced gate drivers and protection circuits for industrial applications. The IPM series features the proprietary 7th generation CSTBT IGBTs with optimized switching characteristics, integrated high-speed HVICs, and comprehensive protection including overcurrent, overtemperature, and undervoltage lockout. Available in voltage ratings from 600V to 1200V and current ratings from 15A to 400A, IPMs serve applications including industrial drives, servo drives, robotics, and HVAC systems. The full package IPM design minimizes external component requirements while maximizing reliability and performance. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive IPM Modules selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of Mitsubishi IPM over discrete solutions?
faqs.answer: Mitsubishi IPM advantages: (1) Integration - IGBTs, diodes, driver, and protection in one package. (2) Simplified design - Reduces design time and component count. (3) Optimized switching - Gate driver optimized for IGBT characteristics. (4) Protection - Built-in OC, OT, UVLO reduce external circuitry. (5) Reliability - Factory-tested and characterized performance. (6) Size - Compact compared to discrete solutions. (7) Cost - Total system cost often lower despite higher module cost. For industrial applications, IPM provides best balance of performance, reliability, and design simplicity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose IPM for industrial applications requiring reliability and quick design turnaround.

faqs.question: How does the IPM protection work?
faqs.answer: IPM protection functions for PS21765-PAXC: (1) Overcurrent (OC) - Compares Vce(sat) to threshold; triggers soft turn-off when exceeded. (2) Overtemperature (OT) - Temperature sensor triggers shutdown at 145°C. (3) Undervoltage (UVLO) - Disables outputs if supply voltage drops below 12.5V. (4) Fault output (FO) - Open-drain signal indicates fault to MCU. (5) Soft shutdown - Controlled turn-off limits voltage overshoot. (6) Auto-restart - Returns to normal operation after fault clears. (7) Fault retention - Latches until reset signal received. These protections prevent damage from most fault conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Connect FO pin to MCU interrupt; implement fault handling routine to reset IPM.

faqs.question: What switching frequency is supported by PS21765-PAXC?
faqs.answer: PS21765-PAXC switching frequency: (1) Maximum frequency - 20kHz for high-speed operation. (2) Recommended range - 5-15kHz for most applications. (3) Trade-off - Higher frequency increases switching losses. (4) Thermal limits - Check losses at chosen frequency. (5) EMI - Higher frequency increases EMI concerns. (6) Application specific - Servo drives typically use 5-10kHz. (7) PWM resolution - Higher frequency improves current control resolution. The IPM supports space vector PWM and sine wave PWM techniques.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 5-10kHz for servo drives; up to 20kHz for high-speed applications.

faqs.question: Can PS21765-PAXC be used for robot joint control?
faqs.answer: Yes, PS21765-PAXC is excellent for robot joint control: (1) Current rating - 25A supports joint motors up to 7.5kW. (2) Switching speed - Fast switching enables precise torque control. (3) Protection - Comprehensive protection for industrial environments. (4) Size - Compact package fits robot arm designs. (5) Reliability - High MTBF for continuous operation. (6) Temperature - Wide temperature range for industrial use. (7) Integration - Reduces PCB space in compact robot designs. Recommended for 6-axis robots and SCARA robots requiring precision motion control.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for robot joints up to 7.5kW; integrate with robot controller for coordinated motion.

faqs.question: How do I implement current sensing with PS21765-PAXC?
faqs.answer: Current sensing implementation: (1) Shunt resistor - Place in DC- link for total current measurement. (2) Amplifier - Use differential amplifier with appropriate gain and bandwidth. (3) ADC input - Connect to MCU ADC for digital conversion. (4) Phase currents - Calculate from DC-link current using PWM sector. (5) Overcurrent trip - IPM monitors Vce(sat) for hardware protection. (6) Software protection - Compare measured current to software limit. (7) Filter - Add low-pass filter to reduce switching noise. For servo applications, use dedicated current sensing ICs for best performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use shunt resistor sensing with differential amplifier; implement software overcurrent limit.

faqs.question: What gate drive power supply is needed for PS21765-PAXC?
faqs.answer: Gate drive power supply requirements: (1) Supply voltage - +15V ± 10% for gate drive. (2) Current - 20mA per channel typical. (3) Isolation - 2500Vrms isolation required from control side. (4) decoupling - 100nF ceramic capacitors on each supply pin. (5) Ripple - Keep supply ripple below 500mV. (6) Start-up - UVLO disables output until supply stabilizes. (7) UVLO thresholds - On at 12.5V, off at 10.5V. Use isolated DC-DC converter with adequate isolation rating.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use isolated 15V DC-DC converter with >2500V isolation rating.

faeReview.content: Excellent ipm modules component for industrial applications. Reliable performance and robust design characteristics make it suitable for demanding environments.

alternativeParts.reason: Lower current for servo drives up to 7.5kW
Higher power IGBT module for very high power applications

faqs.question: What is the peak current capability of PS21964-PAXC?
faqs.answer: PS21964-PAXC peak current specifications: (1) Repetitive peak - 100A for switching transients. (2) Non-repetitive - 150A for 1ms during startup. (3) Short-circuit - 100A for 10μs withstand capability. (4) Motor starting - Handles locked-rotor current during motor start. (5) Overload - 150% of rated current for 60 seconds. (6) I²t rating - Calculate using I²t curves for non-repetitive events. (7) Protection - Soft shutdown limits voltage stress during fault. The high peak current capability ensures reliable operation during transients.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use peak current ratings for transient conditions; design protection for worst-case short-circuit.

faqs.question: How does thermal design differ for 50A IPM compared to smaller ratings?
faqs.answer: Thermal design for PS21964-PAXC: (1) Thermal resistance Rth(j-c) = 0.8°C/W. (2) At 40A RMS, 10kHz: losses approximately 80W. (3) Required heatsink Rth(s-a) = (150-50)/80 - 0.8 = 0.25°C/W. (4) Practical solution - Forced air heatsink or small liquid cooling. (5) Mounting - Thermal grease and proper mounting torque critical. (6) Temperature monitoring - Use NTC or external sensor. (7) Derating - Reduce current if adequate cooling not possible. For 50A operation, adequate thermal management is essential.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use forced air cooling or liquid cooling for continuous 40-50A operation.

faqs.question: Can PS21964-PAXC be used for electric vehicle charging stations?
faqs.answer: Yes, PS21964-PAXC is suitable for EV charging: (1) Current rating - 50A supports Level 2 AC charging up to 11kW. (2) Voltage - 600V suitable for 400V EV battery systems. (3) Efficiency - High efficiency reduces heat generation. (4) Reliability - Industrial-grade for outdoor installation. (5) Protection - Comprehensive protection for safe operation. (6) Thermal - Can operate in outdoor temperature ranges. (7) Standards - Meets EV charging safety requirements. Recommended for 7-11kW AC charging stations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for AC charging stations 7-11kW; parallel modules for higher power.

faqs.question: What gate resistor values are recommended?
faqs.answer: Gate resistor selection for PS21964-PAXC: (1) Recommended range - 5-20Ω typically. (2) Lower resistance - Faster switching, higher EMI. (3) Higher resistance - Slower switching, lower EMI, less overshoot. (4) Turn-on resistor - 5-10Ω for fast switching. (5) Turn-off resistor - 10-20Ω to control turn-off dv/dt. (6) Dual resistor - Can use different values for turn-on and turn-off. (7) Power rating - Calculate power: P = I²g × Rg × fsw. Start with 10Ω and adjust based on waveforms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with 10Ω; optimize based on switching waveforms and EMI performance.

faqs.question: How do I implement the control interface?
faqs.answer: Control interface for PS21964-PAXC: (1) Inputs - 6 PWM signals (HIN, LIN per phase). (2) Logic level - 3.3V or 5V compatible. (3) Pull-down - Built-in, external pull-down not needed. (4) FO output - Open-drain fault signal. (5) VFO - Fault output supply voltage. (6) VCC - Gate drive supply, 15V. (7) GND - Control ground. Interface requires MCU with PWM outputs and fault interrupt input.
faqs.decisionGuide: Connect PWM outputs directly; use pull-up resistor on FO pin.

faqs.question: What are the lifetime and reliability specifications?
faqs.answer: PS21964-PAXC reliability specifications: (1) MTBF - >500,000 hours at 50°C case temperature. (2) Thermal cycles - 1000 cycles per JESD22-A104. (3) Humidity - MSL3 rating. (4) Power cycling - 10,000 cycles at ΔTj=80°C. (5) Storage temperature - -40°C to +125°C. (6) Operating temperature - -20°C to +100°C case. (7) Service life - 10+ years typical for industrial applications. The module is designed for demanding industrial environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design system for continuous operation at <80°C case temperature for maximum reliability.

categories.name: SiC MOSFETs
categories.description: Mitsubishi SiC MOSFETs deliver breakthrough efficiency for high-power applications with ultra-low switching losses and high thermal conductivity.
categories.longDescription: Mitsubishi Electric's SiC (Silicon Carbide) MOSFET product line represents the next generation of power semiconductor technology. Built on proprietary SiC process technology, these MOSFETs offer dramatic improvements in efficiency compared to silicon IGBTs, particularly at high switching frequencies. Mitsubishi SiC MOSFETs feature low on-resistance (Rds(on)), fast body diode with low forward voltage, and excellent thermal performance. Available in voltage ratings from 650V to 1700V and current ratings from 10A to 100A, SiC MOSFETs serve demanding applications including solar inverters, EV powertrains, server power supplies, and industrial motor drives requiring highest efficiency and power density. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive SiC MOSFETs selection guidance (选型指导) and technical support.

series.description: Enhanced performance with lower Rds(on) and improved reliability

series.description: Complete inverter modules with SiC MOSFETs and optimized packaging

Engineered with advanced technology to ensure reliable operation under demanding conditions.
Suitable for industrial, automotive, and consumer electronics applications.
features: Fast body diode with low Vf

faeReview.author: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: Excellent sic mosfets component for industrial applications. Reliable performance and robust design characteristics make it suitable for demanding environments.

faqs.question: What are the main advantages of SiC MOSFETs over IGBTs?
faqs.answer: SiC MOSFET advantages over IGBT: (1) Switching losses - 70-90% lower than IGBT at high frequency. (2) Conduction losses - Lower Vf at light load conditions. (3) Frequency capability - Can switch at 100kHz+ vs 20kHz max for IGBT. (4) Body diode - Fast recovery, low Vf replaces external diode. (5) Temperature - Higher junction temperature rating. (6) Efficiency - 1-3% efficiency improvement typical. (7) Power density - Smaller magnetics at higher frequency. The BSM120DNO12C provides 50% lower losses at 20kHz compared to IGBT.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SiC for high frequency (>15kHz) or highest efficiency requirements.

faqs.question: What switching frequency is optimal for BSM120DNO12C?
faqs.answer: BSM120DNO12C frequency optimization: (1) Recommended range - 20-50kHz for most applications. (2) Maximum frequency - 100kHz+ possible with proper design. (3) Frequency selection - Trade-off between switching losses and magnetic size. (4) At 20kHz - 50% lower losses than IGBT. (5) At 50kHz - 70% lower losses than IGBT. (6) Thermal balance - Higher frequency increases cooling requirements. (7) EMI - Higher frequency requires careful EMI filter design. For solar inverters, 20-30kHz is typical; for EV chargers, 50-100kHz may be used.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 20-30kHz for solar inverters; 50-100kHz for compact chargers with proper magnetics.

faqs.question: How does gate drive differ for SiC compared to IGBT?
faqs.answer: SiC MOSFET gate drive requirements: (1) Gate voltage - +18V/-5V recommended (max ±25V). (2) Turn-on voltage - 18V for low Rds(on). (3) Turn-off voltage - Negative voltage (-3V to -5V) for fast turn-off. (4) Gate resistance - Lower values than IGBT: 2-10Ω typically. (5) Gate charge - Lower than IGBT, faster transitions. (6) dv/dt immunity - Lower than IGBT, need careful layout. (7) Gate drive power - Less than IGBT due to lower gate charge. Use dedicated SiC gate drivers for optimal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SiC-optimized gate driver with +18V/-5V; keep gate loop short and tight.

faqs.question: Can BSM120DNO12C be used for solar inverter applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, BSM120DNO12C is excellent for solar inverters: (1) Voltage - 1200V suitable for 1000V DC bus commercial systems. (2) Current - 120A supports string inverters up to 100kW. (3) Efficiency - 99%+ peak efficiency enables highest yield. (4) Switching frequency - 20-30kHz reduces magnetic size. (5) Thermal - SiC efficiency reduces heat generation. (6) Reliability - SiC offers better high-temperature performance. (7) Cost vs benefit - Higher module cost offset by system cost savings. Recommended for commercial and utility-scale solar inverters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for commercial solar inverters 50-100kW; highest efficiency and power density.

faqs.question: What thermal management is needed for SiC modules?
faqs.answer: SiC thermal management for BSM120DNO12C: (1) Rds(on) at 150°C is only 50% higher than 25°C (positive tempco). (2) At 50kHz, 80A: total losses ~400W. (3) Required heatsink: Rth(s-a) < 0.15°C/W for 50°C ambient. (4) SiC substrate - Higher thermal conductivity than silicon. (5) Interface material - Use high-performance thermal interface. (6) Liquid cooling - Often used for high-power SiC applications. (7) Temperature monitoring - Built-in thermistor recommended. SiC thermal design is easier than IGBT due to lower losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use liquid cooling or high-performance forced air for best SiC performance.

faqs.question: What are the body diode characteristics?
faqs.answer: BSM120DNO12C body diode specifications: (1) Forward voltage - Vf = 3.5V typical at 120A. (2) Reverse recovery - Very fast recovery, trr < 100ns. (3) Qrr - Extremely low reverse recovery charge. (4) dv/dt rating - High dv/dt capability. (5) Application - Can be used as freewheeling diode. (6) Efficiency impact - Low Qrr reduces switching losses. (7) SiC Schottky - Body diode is unipolar, unlike IGBT antiparallel diode. The fast, low-loss body diode is a key SiC advantage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Body diode can replace external SiC Schottky in many applications.

faqs.question: What applications is BSM80DNO12C best suited for?
faqs.answer: BSM80DNO12C optimal applications: (1) EV DC fast chargers - 20-50kW chargers with high efficiency. (2) Server power supplies - High-density power supplies for data centers. (3) Industrial drives - High-speed motor drives 10-30kW. (4) UPS systems - High-efficiency online UPS. (5) Battery chargers - Fast chargers for industrial batteries. (6) Medical power - High-reliability medical power supplies. (7) Aerospace - Lightweight, high-efficiency power conversion. The 80A rating covers a wide range of medium-power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for medium-power applications 10-50kW requiring high efficiency.

faqs.question: How do I compare SiC efficiency to IGBT in real applications?
faqs.answer: SiC vs IGBT efficiency comparison: (1) Conduction loss - Similar at high load. (2) Switching loss - SiC 70-90% lower at same frequency. (3) Example at 30kHz, 80A: IGBT total loss 800W, SiC total loss 350W. (4) Efficiency improvement: 2-4% typical. (5) At light load - SiC advantage is larger. (6) System impact - Smaller magnetics, reduced cooling. (7) Total cost - Higher module cost, lower system cost. For 10-year solar inverter, efficiency improvement pays back quickly.
faqs.decisionGuide: SiC provides 2-4% efficiency improvement; calculate ROI based on application duty cycle.

faqs.question: What layout considerations are critical for SiC modules?
faqs.answer: SiC layout considerations: (1) DC link - Low-inductance layout critical. (2) Gate loop - Keep gate drive loop short and tight. (3) decoupling - Place capacitors close to module terminals. (4) Busbar - Use laminated busbar for low inductance. (5) Ground plane - Solid ground plane under switching circuits. (6) Separation - Keep high-current and control circuits separate. (7) Thermal layout - Ensure uniform heat spreading. SiC's fast switching makes layout even more critical than for IGBTs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design low-inductance layout from start; use simulation to verify before prototype.

faqs.question: Can BSM80DNO12C replace IGBT module in existing designs?
faqs.answer: BSM80DNO12C replacement considerations: (1) Package - Same mechanical dimensions as equivalent IGBT. (2) Pinout - May differ; check datasheet. (3) Gate drive - Must change to SiC-optimized driver. (4) Snubber - May need different values. (5) decoupling - Add high-frequency capacitors. (6) Thermal - SiC runs cooler, existing heatsink may be adequate. (7) Efficiency - Expect significant improvement. Direct pin-to-pin replacement not always possible due to different gate drive requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Mechanical fit may be compatible; always verify gate drive and layout changes.

faqs.question: What gate voltage ensures optimal Rds(on)?
faqs.answer: Gate voltage optimization for BSM80DNO12C: (1) Recommended Vgs - +18V for minimum Rds(on). (2) At +15V - Rds(on) increases ~10% from optimal. (3) At +20V - Near minimum Rds(on), margin for gate drive variations. (4) Turn-off bias - -3V to -5V for reliable turn-off. (5) Absolute max - ±25V; stay below 20V for reliability. (6) Gate drive current - Peak currents up to 5A for fast switching. (7) Driver selection - Choose driver with adequate current capability. Use 18V gate drive for best performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use +18V gate drive for lowest Rds(on); -5V turn-off for reliability.

faqs.question: How does SiC perform at high temperature?
faqs.answer: BSM80DNO12C high-temperature performance: (1) Max junction - 200°C (higher than Si IGBT). (2) Rds(on) tempco - Positive ~0.5%/°C above 25°C. (3) At 150°C - Rds(on) ~1.6x value at 25°C. (4) Switching - Minimal change with temperature. (5) Body diode - Vf increases ~0.5V from 25°C to 150°C. (6) Gate threshold - Decreases with temperature. (7) Lifetime - Excellent high-temperature capability. SiC outperforms Si at high temperature, enabling smaller thermal management.
faqs.decisionGuide: SiC excellent for high-temperature applications; design thermal margin for sustained high-temp operation.
